key_insight: Local chemical order and random solid-solution effects in WTaCrV change radiation damage formation, but both can preserve mechanical strength better than a chemically homogenized model under irradiation.
engineering_relevance: Chemical ordering and lattice distortion are promising design levers for improving irradiation tolerance in refractory high-entropy alloys for advanced nuclear reactor structures.
method: Atomistic comparison of three configurations: Average-Atom (AA), random solid-solution (RSS), and local chemical order (LCO)
New interatomic potentials used to simulate collision cascades and primary radiation damage
Mechanical response assessed after irradiation, including strength and flow stress, with deformation twinning effects
evidence_signal: Collision cascade simulations show Frenkel pair production follows NRSS > NLCO > NAA at the same dose, while RSS and LCO models retain strength much better than the AA model.
watch_item: The conclusions depend on atomistic modeling and newly developed potentials, so transferability to real microstructures and experimental irradiation conditions still needs validation.
paper_title: Primary damage and mechanical degradation of WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy: effects of solid-solution and chemical ordering
